title: The Couch

jokes.text: Love seat is interesting. Some guy thought: 'If they won't sit closer to us why not just shorten the furniture?'
jokes.setup: Jerry commenting on love seats
jokes.punchline: Absurd business logic - solve social distance with smaller furniture
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Why not call it the half-conscious-deadbeat-with-no-job-home-all-day...eating-Cheetos and-watching-TV recliner?
jokes.setup: Criticism of La-Z-Boy name being too flattering
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest alternative name that escalates to specific loser imagery
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity with specific, vivid imagery. Very quotable Seinfeld-style rant

jokes.text: And it goes back so far, that thing. I mean, go to bed already. It's over. You're wiped.
jokes.setup: La-Z-Boy reclines extremely far back
jokes.punchline: Treating furniture position as admission of defeat
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the La-Z-Boy bit with typical Jerry attitude

jokes.text: So she got you to join a book club? I got a feeling I'm gonna be much smarter than you pretty soon.
jokes.setup: George joining a book club to get smarter
jokes.punchline: George's immediate overconfidence about intellectual superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect George character moment - instant arrogance from minimal effort

jokes.text: Well, I think that statement alone reflects your burgeoning intelligence.
jokes.setup: George's claim about getting smarter
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic use of 'burgeoning' to mock George's pretensions
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with sophisticated vocabulary ironically deployed

jokes.text: Breakfast at Tiffany's. Ninety pages.
jokes.setup: Discussion of George's first book club book
jokes.punchline: George immediately focuses on how short the book is
jokes.explanation: Perfect George - reduces classic literature to page count, maximum economy

jokes.text: Oh, sure, Truman Capote. - He's a great writer. - Oh, yeah. - Ever read anything by him? - No. You? No.
jokes.setup: Both praising Truman Capote as a great writer
jokes.punchline: Neither has read anything by him
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - builds up authority then deflates it perfectly

jokes.text: I love this couch. The best part is it doesn't fold out...so no one can sleep over.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing what he loves about his new couch
jokes.punchline: Anti-social feature is the 'best part'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Jerry - celebrates antisocial features as benefits. Dark but relatable

jokes.text: In a way, I feel like I'm getting a new couch. Yeah, so do I.
jokes.setup: George excited about getting Jerry's old couch
jokes.punchline: Jerry agrees he's also getting a new couch despite giving his away
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist logic - both feel they're getting something new in opposite transaction

jokes.text: I can't wait to get me a fella and make my own pie.
jokes.setup: Kramer describing the make-your-own pizza concept
jokes.punchline: Jerry's deliberately awkward/rural phrasing about getting 'a fella'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay with 'fella' but not particularly sharp or memorable

jokes.text: They got a duck there, you think you died and went to heaven.
jokes.setup: Kramer praising Poppie's restaurant food
jokes.punchline: Extreme metaphor for how good the duck is
jokes.explanation: Standard Kramer enthusiasm with clichéd metaphor, but in character

jokes.text: What do you think, Lainey? Well, I don't know. I'll have to sit on it. Oh, no, I don't want anyone sitting on it.
jokes.setup: Elaine says she needs to 'sit on' the decision about the couch
jokes.punchline: Jerry takes it literally and objects to actual sitting
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay setup with Jerry's obsessive couch protection

jokes.text: I'll see you chumps later.
jokes.setup: George leaving with the movers
jokes.punchline: Calling his friends 'chumps' while being the biggest loser
jokes.explanation: Typical George obliviousness - calling others chumps while mooching furniture

jokes.text: Did you offer those guys a drink? No. Should I have? - What kind of a person are you? - I don't know.
jokes.setup: Elaine outraged Jerry didn't offer movers drinks
jokes.punchline: Jerry's complete uncertainty about his own character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry response - existential uncertainty about basic human decency

jokes.text: Do you date moving men? You wanna know what I said? I can't wait. I do now.
jokes.setup: Moving man's pickup line to Elaine
jokes.punchline: Elaine's supposedly clever response
jokes.explanation: Moderately clever comeback but not exceptional, fits Elaine's character

jokes.text: Well, they're real men, Jerry. They get sweaty. So anyone sweaty in your house has to be offered a drink? Yes.
jokes.setup: Elaine's logic about offering drinks to sweaty workers
jokes.punchline: Jerry reducing it to absurd universal rule about sweat
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of social etiquette rules

jokes.text: It is so succulent, so succulent.
jokes.setup: Poppie describing the duck
jokes.punchline: Overly sensual repetition of 'succulent'
jokes.explanation: Establishes Poppie's character quirk with food obsession, becomes running element

jokes.text: My mother was taken from my house...by the communists in the middle of the night...when I was 10 years old. She was sent to a slave-labor camp...where she labored for 12 years. Finally, they released her...and she was on a boat to America to reunite with us. But she was served some bad fish, and she died on the high seas.
jokes.setup: Elaine's innocent comment about Poppie's mother being proud
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark, detailed tragic backstory as response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation - completely derails casual conversation with tragic overshare

jokes.text: So, what's good tonight?
jokes.setup: Poppie's extremely heavy story about his mother's death
jokes.punchline: Jerry's completely casual transition to ordering food
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - Jerry's oblivious transition after devastating story is brilliantly uncomfortable

jokes.text: The owner contributes money...to those fanatical anti-abortion groups. So you won't eat the pizza? No way.
jokes.setup: Discussion of pizza place owner's political donations
jokes.punchline: Elaine's absolute boycott over political stance
jokes.explanation: Sets up the abortion conflict that will explode later, character-consistent for Elaine

jokes.text: Well, perhaps we should inquire.
jokes.setup: Casual suggestion to ask about Poppie's abortion stance
jokes.punchline: Jerry's formal phrasing for stirring up explosive controversy
jokes.explanation: Jerry's mischievous formality before dropping a social bomb - great setup for disaster

jokes.text: It is more succulent than even I had hoped.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about the duck before the abortion question
jokes.punchline: Poppie's escalating duck obsession before political explosion
jokes.explanation: Callback to Poppie's food obsession, creates dramatic irony before the fight

jokes.text: Poppie, I was just curious. Where do you stand on abortion?
jokes.setup: Casual dinner conversation
jokes.punchline: Jerry dropping the most divisive possible question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry move - casually detonating social bomb with scientific curiosity

jokes.text: Well, Poppie, I think differently. And what gives you the right to do that? The Supreme Court gives me the right to do that!
jokes.setup: Poppie's absolutist anti-abortion stance
jokes.punchline: Elaine invoking Supreme Court in restaurant fight
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - brings constitutional law into restaurant dinner fight

jokes.text: Well, I'm certainly glad I brought it up.
jokes.setup: Complete restaurant meltdown over abortion question
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry satisfaction with the chaos he caused
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry button - deadpan satisfaction at social destruction he engineered. Exceptional economy and character

jokes.text: And I have to say...it was pretty much all my fault.
jokes.setup: Jerry recounting the restaurant disaster
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of deliberate chaos-making
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry moment - false modesty about deliberate troublemaking

jokes.text: I say how's the book coming? Oh, pretty good. So, what's it about? Well, it's about Holly Golightly. Holly Golightly. Yeah, she's quite a character. Haven't read a page, have you? No.
jokes.setup: Jerry testing George's book club progress with specific questions
jokes.punchline: George's immediate confession after trying to fake it
jokes.explanation: Classic George - attempts deception but crumbles instantly under questioning

jokes.text: You're not very bright, are you? No, I'm not. I would like to be, but I'm not.
jokes.setup: Jerry's blunt assessment of George's intelligence
jokes.punchline: George's completely honest, defeated self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George moment - complete self-awareness and acceptance of his limitations with touching honesty

jokes.text: Why don't you rent the movie? Why don't I rent the movie? You see? This is when I like you.
jokes.setup: George's academic crisis over unread book
jokes.punchline: Jerry liking George only when he finds shortcuts to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jerry appreciating George's laziness and corner-cutting instincts

jokes.text: Do you know I've been using the same bottle of shampoo for a year? And I shampoo every day. So, what do you think of my conversation? Not much.
jokes.setup: Elaine making awkward conversation about shampoo
jokes.punchline: Carl's brutal honest assessment of her conversation skills
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Elaine's desperate small talk met with blunt rejection

jokes.text: You don't have any furniture? No, I hate furniture. I can't look at it. Oh, I can understand that.
jokes.setup: Carl explaining why he has no furniture
jokes.punchline: Elaine's casual acceptance of furniture phobia
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - treating furniture hatred as reasonable phobia

jokes.text: A pretty good date, huh? Yeah, no heavy lifting.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking about the quality of their date
jokes.punchline: Carl's literal interpretation referring to his moving job
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay connecting his job to dating experience

jokes.text: A bottle of wine and a five-alarm chili? They're trying to kill Poppie? Why? Don't they know I have...a gastrointestinal disorder? If I would have any of this, I would die. Then Poppie's no good to anyone. This is a sick, sick joke on Poppie.
jokes.setup: Jerry and Elaine's peace offering gifts to hospitalized Poppie
jokes.punchline: Poppie interpreting wine and spicy food as assassination attempt
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Poppie's paranoid interpretation of innocent gifts as murder plot

jokes.text: I'm in love! This is it, Jerry! This is it!
jokes.setup: Elaine's relationship with Carl the moving man
jokes.punchline: Extreme romantic declaration about casual hookup
jokes.explanation: Standard Elaine romantic overreaction, sets up for later disappointment

jokes.text: No games? What is the point of dating without games? How do you know if you're winning or losing?
jokes.setup: Elaine praising Carl for not playing dating games
jokes.punchline: Jerry treating dating as competitive sport requiring scorekeeping
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jerry observation - reducing romance to game theory with perfect logic

jokes.text: Well, I'm sure he's pro-choice. How do you know? Because he...Well...He's just so good-looking.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Carl's abortion stance
jokes.punchline: Elaine assuming political views based on attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Elaine's shallow political profiling based on looks

jokes.text: Maybe we could call them and ask them to return it. Sorry, we can't do that. Well, maybe they're done with it. I could go pick it up. I don't think so. It doesn't work that way.
jokes.setup: George desperately trying to get Breakfast at Tiffany's movie
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and inappropriate suggestions to video store
jokes.explanation: Good George escalation - proposing increasingly unreasonable video store policies

jokes.text: Excuse me, are you Joe Temple? Yes. Yes, you don't know me. My name is George Costanza. Did you happen to rent Breakfast at Tiffany's?
jokes.setup: George's academic desperation over the movie
jokes.punchline: Stalking random customer to their home for movie access
jokes.explanation: Perfect George desperation - crossing all social boundaries for lazy academic shortcut

jokes.text: Kramer, what is this? What is what? This puddle on my sofa! What puddle? That puddle! I don't know. Is it...? Could it...? Could he have...? It is! Poppie peed on my sofa!
jokes.setup: Jerry discovering mysterious puddle on his new couch
jokes.punchline: Horrified realization that Poppie urinated on his precious new furniture
jokes.explanation: Great escalating horror as Jerry pieces together the violation of his pristine couch

jokes.text: My new sofa! Poppie peed on my new sofa! I'm sure it'll come out. I don't care if it comes out. I can't sit on that anymore.
jokes.setup: Jerry's horror at the couch violation
jokes.punchline: Jerry's complete psychological contamination despite potential cleaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry neurosis - psychological damage beyond physical cleaning

jokes.text: Just a natural human function. Happens to be on my sofa...instead of in the toilet, where it would normally be.
jokes.setup: Kramer's casual dismissal of the couch peeing
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry acknowledgment while highlighting the location problem
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry deadpan - accepting the biological reality while noting the venue issue

jokes.text: Well, as I say, the pinkeye made my vision quite blurry.
jokes.setup: George asking why Joe didn't read the book
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse for not reading while renting movie
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist excuse but not particularly memorable or character-specific

jokes.text: She got impregnated by her troglodytic half-brother...and decided to have an abortion.
jokes.setup: Elaine casually mentioning thinking about a friend
jokes.punchline: Extremely disturbing story designed to test Carl's abortion stance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing test scenario - combines incest and abortion for maximum political litmus test

jokes.text: You know, someday...we're gonna get enough people on the Supreme Court to change that law.
jokes.setup: Elaine's extreme abortion scenario
jokes.punchline: Carl's anti-choice stance despite his good looks
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Elaine's looks-based political profiling - proves her completely wrong

jokes.text: You know what I love? How there's two nuts named after people: Hazel and Filbert.
jokes.setup: George eating nuts at Joe's house
jokes.punchline: Random trivia about personified nuts
jokes.explanation: Typical George random observation - mildly interesting trivia delivered inappropriately

jokes.text: You can't put cucumbers on a pizza. Why not? I like cucumbers. That's not a pizza. It will taste terrible. But that's the idea. Make your own pie. Yes, but we cannot give the people the right...to choose any topping they want. Now, on this issue, there can be no debate. What gives you the right to tell me how I make my pie? Because it's a pizza. It's not a pizza till it comes out of the oven. It's a pizza the moment you put your fists in the dough. No, it isn't! Yes, it is!
jokes.setup: Kramer putting cucumbers on pizza at make-your-own pizza restaurant
jokes.punchline: Philosophical argument about pizza identity escalating to shouting match
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parallel to abortion debate using pizza toppings - same authoritarian vs. choice structure

jokes.text: I'll pause it, okay?
jokes.setup: George complaining about family conversation during movie
jokes.punchline: Joe's reluctant accommodation of rude houseguest
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe moment - polite host dealing with George's inappropriate demands

jokes.text: So who is this guy? He's in some book club. And what's he doing here? Cheating on his test.
jokes.setup: Wife asking about George's presence
jokes.punchline: Reducing George's academic fraud to elementary school terms
jokes.explanation: Good reduction of George's situation to childish terms

jokes.text: You didn't save it. I had the arm.
jokes.setup: George and child fighting over couch seat
jokes.punchline: George invoking playground rules with a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect George - competing with child using children's rules

jokes.text: Look! Look what you did! You got grape juice all over our couch. You ruined our couch!
jokes.setup: George spilling grape juice during couch fight
jokes.punchline: George destroying the Temple's couch while trying to watch movie to avoid reading
jokes.explanation: Good ironic escalation - George's laziness leads to property damage

jokes.text: Did you tell him it was peed on? He said he doesn't care. He'll just turn the cushion over.
jokes.setup: Carl asking about informing George about the couch condition
jokes.punchline: George's gross pragmatic solution to urine-soaked furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect George pragmatism - disgusting but logical solution shows his standards

jokes.text: All I've got is grape juice. Throw it.
jokes.setup: Carl asking for a drink
jokes.punchline: Only having grape juice after couch-staining incident
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the couch-staining incident - grape juice becomes cursed beverage

jokes.text: The couch!
jokes.setup: Carl throwing grape juice to Elaine
jokes.punchline: More grape juice destroying more furniture
jokes.explanation: Escalation of couch destruction theme - grape juice as furniture destroyer

jokes.text: Well, not really. After all, she did get together with George Peppard. I mean, Fred. George, Fred's gay.
jokes.setup: George discussing Holly Golightly's independence in book club
jokes.punchline: George confusing movie actor with character, then learning character is gay
jokes.explanation: Good George moment - movie knowledge fails him even after watching, shows his confusion

tmdb_overview: Freedom-of-choice issues disrupt Elaine's new relationship and Kramer's business; George watches a video instead of reading his book-club book.
